title: The oil market is reaching a ‘tipping point’ that could create problems for stocks, according to this Wall Street legend
summary: Evercore’s Roger Altman said in a CNBC interview on Monday that a sharp rise in crude could destabilize markets before it may trigger “the second big inflation shock of this decade after COVID,” especially if oil prices climb toward $150 a barrel or higher.

summary_cn: Evercore的罗杰·奥尔特曼(Roger Altman)周一在接受CNBC采访时表示，原油价格大幅上涨可能会破坏市场稳定，然后可能引发“继新冠肺炎之后本十年来的第二次重大通胀冲击”。

That was the moment when human beings first unleashed the power of the nucleus in an immense, blinding ball of fire above a gloomy stretch of desert in the Jornada del Muerto basin in New Mexico.
